fundamental_rights: Article 19(1)(a) — Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression (includes right to information)
Article 21 — Right to Life (includes right to know about government decisions affecting life)

laws.name: Right to Information Act, 2005
sections: Section 7 (reply within 30 days; 48 hours if life/liberty at stake)
Section 8 (exemptions — national security, personal privacy etc.)
Section 19 (first appeal to senior officer within 30 days; second appeal to CIC/SIC)
Section 20 (penalty Rs 250/day up to Rs 25,000 for PIO's failure)
laws.summary: Every citizen can request information from any public authority. Information must be provided within 30 days. Fee is Rs 10. BPL applicants: free of charge.
laws.remedy: File RTI with PIO; appeal to First Appellate Authority; second appeal to CIC/SIC

steps: Identify the Public Information Officer (PIO) of the relevant government department
Write a simple application: your name, address, the specific information you want, and application fee (Rs 10 — by postal order or court fee stamp; free for BPL)
Submit by post, in person, or online at rtionline.gov.in (for Central government bodies)
PIO must respond within 30 days (48 hours if the matter involves life or liberty)
If no reply or unsatisfactory: file First Appeal to the First Appellate Authority within 30 days
If still unsatisfied: file Second Appeal to State Information Commission (SIC) or Central Information Commission (CIC) at cic.gov.in
PIO can be penalised Rs 250 per day (max Rs 25,000) for unjustified delays
